[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 樊寮至孝 - Indicates that Fan Liao was extremely filial, a key theme of the story.
  • 内亲早亡 - Refers to the early death of Fan Liao's inner family members, likely close relatives.
  • 患恶肿 - Describes a severe swelling or tumor.
  • 内结成痈 - Means 'internally forming an abscess,' indicating a deep-seated infection.
  • 楚毒难忍 - Refers to unbearable pain.
  • 针灸 - Refers to acupuncture, a traditional Chinese medical practice.
  • 恐痛 - Means 'fearing pain,' indicating Fan Liao's concern for his stepmother's discomfort.
  • 与口于母肿上吮之 - Means 'sucking on the swelling on his mother's body with his mouth,' a dramatic and selfless act of care.
  • 即得小差 - Means 'immediately got slightly better,' indicating a temporary improvement.
  • 脓务数口流出 - Describes the continuous flow of pus from the wound.
  • 便得眠卧安稳 - Means 'was able to sleep peacefully,' indicating relief from pain.
  • 其疮上复得鲤鱼哺之 - Means 'if the wound is fed by a carp,' a metaphorical instruction for healing.
  • 若不得鲤鱼食之，即应死矣 - Means 'if no carp is eaten, then death will ensue,' emphasizing the urgency of the situation.
  • 忧心恐惧 - Describes Fan Liao's emotional state of worry and fear.
  • 仰面向天而叹曰 - Means 'looking up to heaven and sighing,' a gesture of despair and plea.
  • 我之不孝，今乃如此 - Means 'My lack of filial piety has led to this,' indicating self-reproach.
  • 十一月冬冰结凝之时 - Refers to the time when ice is frozen in November, a seasonal detail.
  • 何由得此鱼食 - Means 'how can I get this fish to eat,' expressing Fan Liao's dilemma.
  • 抱母头而别 - Means 'embracing his mother's head and parting,' a poignant moment.
  • 出入行哭 - Describes Fan Liao's behavior of crying as he goes in and out.
  • 悲啼泣泪 - Means 'crying and shedding tears,' emphasizing his emotional state.
  • 天若怜我，愿鱼感出 - Means 'If heaven pities me, may the fish be moved to appear,' a plea to the heavens.
  • 脱衣覆冰之上 - Means 'removing his clothes and lying on the ice,' a dramatic act of self-sacrifice.
  • 赤体卧冰上 - Means 'lying naked on the ice,' further emphasizing his determination.
  • 感出鲤鱼一双 - Means 'two carps appeared,' the fulfillment of his plea.
  • 心生欢悦 - Means 'joy arose in his heart,' indicating relief and happiness.
  • 将归与母食之 - Means 'bringing them back to feed his mother,' a continuation of his care.
  • 及哺之于疮上 - Means 'feeding them to the wound,' the literal act of healing.
  • 即得差矣 - Means 'immediately got better,' indicating complete recovery.
  • 命得长远，延年益寿 - Means 'life became long, and he lived a long time,' indicating a prolonged lifespan.
  • 乃得一百一十而终也 - Means 'he lived to be 110 years old and then died,' indicating a very long life.
  • 樊寮至孝，松柏终不改易 - Means 'Fan Liao's filial piety was as unchanging as the pine and cypress trees,' a metaphor for his enduring virtue.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 樊寮 - The name of the protagonist, Fan Liao, who is the subject of the story.
  • 至孝 - A high standard of filial piety, a key virtue in Confucian ethics.
  • 恶肿 - A severe swelling, often associated with illness or injury.
  • 痈 - An abscess, a localized collection of pus.
  • 楚毒 - Refers to severe pain, often associated with illness or injury.
  • 针灸 - Acupuncture, a traditional Chinese medical practice.
  • 恐痛 - Fearing pain, indicating concern for the well-being of others.
  • 与口于母肿上吮之 - Sucking on the swelling on his mother's body with his mouth, a dramatic and selfless act of care.
  • 即得小差 - Immediately got slightly better, indicating a temporary improvement.
  • 脓务数口流出 - Continuous flow of pus from the wound, indicating a severe infection.
  • 便得眠卧安稳 - Able to sleep peacefully, indicating relief from pain.
  • 其疮上复得鲤鱼哺之 - If the wound is fed by a carp, a metaphorical instruction for healing.
  • 若不得鲤鱼食之，即应死矣 - If no carp is eaten, then death will ensue, emphasizing the urgency of the situation.
  • 忧心恐惧 - Worry and fear, indicating emotional distress.
  • 仰面向天而叹曰 - Looking up to heaven and sighing, a gesture of despair and plea.
  • 我之不孝，今乃如此 - My lack of filial piety has led to this, indicating self-reproach.
  • 十一月冬冰结凝之时 - The time when ice is frozen in November, a seasonal detail.
  • 何由得此鱼食 - How can I get this fish to eat, expressing Fan Liao's dilemma.
  • 抱母头而别 - Embracing his mother's head and parting, a poignant moment.
  • 出入行哭 - Crying as he goes in and out, indicating emotional distress.
  • 悲啼泣泪 - Crying and shedding tears, emphasizing his emotional state.
  • 天若怜我，愿鱼感出 - If heaven pities me, may the fish be moved to appear, a plea to the heavens.
  • 脱衣覆冰之上 - Removing his clothes and lying on the ice, a dramatic act of self-sacrifice.
  • 赤体卧冰上 - Lying naked on the ice, further emphasizing his determination.
  • 感出鲤鱼一双 - Two carps appeared, the fulfillment of his plea.
  • 心生欢悦 - Joy arose in his heart, indicating relief and happiness.
  • 将归与母食之 - Bringing them back to feed his mother, a continuation of his care.
  • 及哺之于疮上 - Feeding them to the wound, the literal act of healing.
  • 即得差矣 - Immediately got better, indicating complete recovery.
  • 命得长远，延年益寿 - Life became long, and he lived a long time, indicating a prolonged lifespan.
  • 乃得一百一十而终也 - He lived to be 110 years old and then died, indicating a very long life.
  • 樊寮至孝，松柏终不改易 - Fan Liao's filial piety was as unchanging as the pine and cypress trees, a metaphor for his enduring virtue.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 哀哀父母，生我劬劳 - Classical expression meaning 'Alas, my parents, who bore me and toiled for me,' expressing deep gratitude and sorrow.
  • 天感至孝 - Means 'Heaven was moved by his filial piety,' attributing his success to his devotion.
  • 非时为生堇菜 - Means 'and caused the purslane to grow out of season,' describing the miraculous event.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 至孝 - Refers to the virtue of filial piety, a central value in Confucian ethics.
  • 天感 - Refers to the belief in divine intervention or heaven's response to human virtue, a common theme in Chinese folklore and literature.

Proper Nouns

  • 张嵩 - The name of the protagonist, a figure in a traditional filial piety story.
  • 陇西 - Refers to Longxi, a region in ancient China, indicating the protagonist's origin.
  • 堇菜 - Refers to purslane, a plant often associated with medicinal properties in Chinese culture.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 葬送亦不用车牛人力 - The burial process did not use carts, oxen, or human labor, which is unusual.
  • 惟夫妇二人，身自负上母棺 - The couple carried their mother's coffin themselves, emphasizing their devotion.
  • 已（以）力擎于车上推之 - They pushed the coffin on a cart using their own strength.
  • 遣妻牵挽而向墓所 - The wife pulled the cart towards the burial site.
  • 其时日有卒风暴雨 - There was a sudden wind and heavy rain during the burial.
  • 泥涂没膝 - The mud was up to their knees, indicating difficult conditions.
  • 然葬送道上，清尘而（不）起 - Despite the mud, no dust rose on the path, which is unusual.
  • 嵩葬既讫，于墓所三年亲自负土培坟 - Zhang Song carried soil to the tomb for three years after his mother's burial.
  • 哭声不绝，头发落尽 - Zhang Song cried continuously and lost all his hair, emphasizing his grief.
  • 天知至孝，于墓所直北起雷之声 - The heavens recognized Zhang Song's filial piety and responded with thunder.
  • 忽有一道风云而来到嵩边 - A cloud and wind suddenly arrived at Zhang Song's side.
  • 抱嵩置墓东八十步 - The cloud and wind carried Zhang Song to a spot 80 steps east of the tomb.
  • 然始霹雳冢开，出其棺 - The tomb opened with a thunderclap, revealing the coffin.
  • 棺额上云 - The coffin lid had a message.
  • 张嵩至孝，通于神明，今日孝感至诚，放母却活延命，更得三十二年。任将归奶侍奉。 - The message on the coffin lid praises Zhang Song's filial piety and states that his mother was revived and granted an additional 32 years of life.
  • 闻者无不嗟叹斯事 - Everyone who heard about this event was moved to admiration.
  • 自至今，未闻斯事 - Such an event has not been heard of since.
  • 天子遂拜嵩为今（金）城太守 - The emperor appointed Zhang Song as the governor of Jin Cheng.
  • 后迁为尚左仆射 - Zhang Song was later promoted to the position of Left Shangpu.
  • 事出《织终传》 - The story is recorded in the *Zhi Zong Zhuan*.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 至孝 - Refers to the highest degree of filial piety.
  • 神明 - Refers to divine beings or spirits.
  • 孝感至诚 - Refers to the power of filial piety to move heaven.
  • 尚左仆射 - A high-ranking official position in ancient China.
  • 《织终传》 - A historical text that records this story.

Proper Nouns

  • 张嵩 - The name of the protagonist, Zhang Song.
  • 尚左仆射 - The title of a high-ranking official.
  • 《织终传》 - The title of the historical text that records this story.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 衣冠不解 - Metaphor for not taking breaks or resting, emphasizing continuous care.
  • 比来梦恶，定知不活 - Conveys a sense of fatalistic acceptance; 'recently having bad dreams, definitely knowing that he will not survive.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 焦华 - Historical figure known for his filial piety during the late Eastern Han period.
  • 尚书左仆射 - High-ranking official position in the Han government, indicating Jiao Hua's status and influence.
  • 长安 - Capital city of the Han dynasty, located in present-day Xi'an, Shaanxi Province.

Proper Nouns

  • 焦华 - Proper noun referring to the historical figure Jiao Hua.
  • 长安 - Proper noun referring to the capital city Chang'an.
  • 尚书左仆射 - Proper noun referring to the high-ranking official position in the Han government.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 患来梦恶何事 - A phrase meaning 'What bad dream did you have?' where '患来' is a colloquial way of asking about a problem or bad experience.
  • 汝欲得活，时得瓜（苽）食之一顿，即活君也 - A phrase meaning 'If you want to survive, you must eat one meal of gourd, and then you will live.'
  • 不得瓜食之，不经旬日，终须死矣 - A phrase meaning 'Without eating gourd, you won't survive for more than ten days.'
  • 仲冬思苽告焦华，父得食之 - A phrase meaning 'In mid-winter, thinking of gourd, Jiao Hua, your father will eat it.'
  • 善好良医，能回丧车起死人 - A phrase meaning 'A good doctor who can bring the dead back to life.'
  • 劈脑出虫，乃为魏武帝所杀 - A phrase meaning 'Cutting open the brain to remove worms, he was killed by Emperor Wei Wu.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 华问父曰 - Refers to Jiao Hua, a historical figure known for his filial piety.
  • 天人下来取我 - Refers to a divine being descending to take someone, a common motif in Chinese mythology.
  • 扁鹊 - Refers to Bian Que, a famous physician from the Warring States period.
  • 魏武帝 - Refers to Cao Cao, a prominent warlord and statesman during the late Eastern Han dynasty.
  • 六国之时 - Refers to the Warring States period, a time of great turmoil and conflict in Chinese history.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 昔有 - A classical opening phrase meaning 'once upon a time' or 'there was.'
  • 善好良医 - Means 'an excellent and skilled physician.'
  • 游行于国 - Means 'traveling throughout the country.'
  • 闻虎（虢）君太子患病 - 'Hearing that the crown prince of the state of Guo was ill.'
  • 死已经八日 - 'Had already died for eight days.'
  • 遂请入见之 - 'He then requested to be allowed to see him.'
  • 还出语人曰 - 'Then he came out and told people.'
  • 太子须（虽）死，犹故可活之 - 'The crown prince, although dead, can still be revived.'
  • 虢君闻之 - 'The ruler of Guo heard this.'
  • 遂唤扁鹊入活太子 - 'He then summoned Bian Que to revive the crown prince.'
  • 遂还得活 - 'And the crown prince was indeed revived.'
  • 大悦 - 'Greatly pleased.'
  • 即赐金银宝璧与鹊 - 'Immediately bestowed gold, silver, jewels, and jade upon Bian Que.'
  • 鹊辞而不受 - 'Bian Que declined to accept them.'
  • 今活吾子 - 'Now that I have revived my son.'
  • 即事不违 - 'Since nothing has gone wrong.'
  • 乃不取受者，何也 - 'Why do you not accept them?'
  • 太子命故未尽 - 'The crown prince's life is not yet finished.'
  • 非臣卒能活得 - 'It is not solely due to my efforts that he was revived.'
  • 去也 - 'He left.'
  • 字公明 - 'His courtesy name was Gongming.'
  • 善好良才 - 'An excellent and talented person.'
  • 尔时六时中旬 - 'At that time, in the middle of the sixth month.'
  • 行过平原 - 'Went past a plain.'
  • 见一年少始可十八九矣 - 'Saw a young man who was about eighteen or nineteen years old.'
  • 在道南刈麦然 - 'Cutting wheat on the south side of the road.'
  • 嗟叹而过 - 'Sighed and passed by.'
  • 何以嗟叹 - 'Why did you sigh?'
  • 汝何字谁 - 'What is your name?'
  • 姓赵，名颜子 - 'My surname is Zhao, and my name is Yanzi.'
  • 向者更无余事 - 'There was nothing else going on earlier.'
  • 直以怜卿好年少 - 'I just felt sorry for you, a young man.'
  • 明日午时忽然猝死 - 'You will suddenly die at noon tomorrow.'
  • 是故嗟叹也 - 'That is why I sighed.'
  • 丈人岂非管辂 - 'Are you not Master Guan Lu?'
  • 我是 - 'I am.'
  • 叩头 - 'Bowed deeply.'
  • 随逐乞命 - 'Followed and begged for mercy.'
  • 命在于天 - 'Fate lies in heaven.'
  • 非我能活 - 'It is not within my power to save you.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 扁鹊 - A legendary physician from the Spring and Autumn period, known for his medical skills and longevity.
  • 虢 - An ancient Chinese state during the Zhou dynasty.
  • 管辂 - A famous diviner and astrologer from the Three Kingdoms period.

Proper Nouns

  • 扁鹊 - Bian Que, a legendary physician.
  • 虢 - The state of Guo.
  • 管辂 - Guan Lu, a famous diviner.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 卿且去 - Polite imperative meaning 'You go ahead' or 'You may leave.'
  • 宜急告父母知 - Means 'You should quickly inform your parents.'
  • 莫令忩忩 - Means 'Do not let them be anxious.'
  • 管聖如 - Likely a respectful address or title for Guan Lu, possibly 'Master Guan' or 'Sir Guan.'
  • 明日午时将死 - Means 'will die tomorrow at noon.'
  • 备觅麋鹿脯一合 - Means 'prepare a jar of deer meat.'
  • 清酒一 - Means 'one bottle of clear wine.'
  • 方始救之 - Means 'only then can he be saved.'
  • 酌合里置酒脯 - Means 'pour the wine and place the deer meat.'
  • 他自取之 - Means 'they will take it themselves.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 颜子 - Refers to Yanzi, likely a common name in ancient China, but without specific historical context here.
  • 管辂 - A historical figure known as Guan Lu, a famous diviner and astrologer during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 麋鹿 - Refers to deer, often associated with nobility and good fortune in Chinese culture.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sentence is a series of instructions given to Yanzi: '卿且去，宜急告父母知，莫令忩忩.'
  • This sentence asks how Guan Lu can show compassion to save the child: '管圣如何忧怜，方可救命.'
  • This sentence instructs the father to return home and prepare the necessary items: '君但且还家，备觅酒脯而待之.'
  • This sentence describes Guan Lu arriving on time and speaking to Yanzi: '管辂明日于期即至，语颜子曰.'
  • This sentence describes a location and an activity from the previous day: '卿昨日刈麦地南头大桑树下，有二人樗蒲博戏.'

Proper Nouns

  • 颜子 - Likely a common name, but could be a specific individual in this context.
  • 管辂 - Guan Lu, a historical figure known for his divination skills.
  • 樗蒲 - A type of dice game played in ancient China.

[8]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 借问于卿 - A formal way of asking someone a question, literally 'if I may ask you.'
  • 但向拜之 - Means 'just bow to them,' indicating a gesture of respect.
  • 其中有一人救卿 - Means 'one of them will save you,' indicating a promise of protection.
  • 吾在此专待卿消息 - Means 'I am waiting for your news here,' expressing anticipation.
  • 颜子行管辂之言 - Means 'Yanzi acted on Guan Lu's advice,' indicating that Yanzi followed the advice of Guan Lu.
  • 乃见二人博戏 - Means 'then he saw two people playing a game,' describing a scene.
  • 侍从非常 - Means 'with extraordinary attendants,' suggesting a supernatural or unusual presence.
  • 颜子遂酌酒与之 - Means 'Yanzi then poured wine for them,' describing an action.
  • 其人得酒即饮 - Means 'the person who received the wine drank it immediately,' indicating quick consumption.
  • 贪博戏不看 - Means 'too engrossed in the game to notice,' suggesting distraction.
  • 饮酒欲尽 - Means 'the wine was almost finished,' indicating the progress of the event.
  • 博戏欲休 - Means 'the game was about to end,' indicating the conclusion of the activity.
  • 北边坐人举头见颜子 - Means 'the person sitting on the north side looked up and saw Yanzi,' describing a reaction.
  • 忽然大恕（怒）曰 - Means 'suddenly became very angry and said,' indicating a sudden change in mood.
  • 小人，我遣你早去 - Means 'fool, I sent you to leave early,' expressing frustration.
  • 因何违他期日 - Means 'why did you violate the agreed time,' questioning the reason for delay.
  • 如午时不去 - Means 'if you don't leave by noon,' setting a deadline.
  • 何由态（能）仍酌我酒来 - Means 'how could you still pour me wine,' expressing disbelief.
  • 颜子再拜，不敢更言 - Means 'Yanzi bowed twice and dared not speak further,' indicating submission.
  • 南边坐人语北边坐人曰 - Means 'the person sitting on the south side said to the person on the north side,' describing a conversation.
  • 凡吃人一食，惭人一色 - Means 'for every meal a person eats, they owe a favor,' indicating a social obligation.
  • 吃人两食，与人着力 - Means 'for every two meals a person eats, they must help someone,' indicating a reciprocal relationship.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 颜子 - Refers to Yanzi, a historical figure known for his wisdom and integrity.
  • 管辂 - Refers to Guan Lu, a famous diviner and astrologer from the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 博戏 - Refers to a board game or gambling game, often associated with social and cultural activities.
  • 侍从非常 - Suggests a supernatural or unusual presence, possibly referring to spirits or deities.

Proper Nouns

  • 颜子 - Yanzi, a historical figure.
  • 管辂 - Guan Lu, a famous diviner and astrologer.
  • 大桑树 - A large mulberry tree, likely a significant location in the story.

[9]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 朝来饮他酒脯 - Colloquial phrase meaning 'drank his wine and meat this morning.'
  • 岂可能活取此人 - Rhetorical question meaning 'how could we possibly take his life?'
  • 文案已定，何由可改 - Formal statement meaning 'the case has been decided, how can it be changed?'
  • 暂借文书看之 - Polite request meaning 'may I borrow the documents to look at them?'
  • 此年始十九，易可改之 - Statement meaning 'he is only nineteen this year, it is easy to change it.'
  • 把笔颠倒句着 - Colloquial phrase meaning 'he reversed the sentence with his pen.'
  • 你合寿十九即死，今放你九十合终也 - Statement meaning 'you were destined to die at nineteen, but now you will live to ninety.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 北边坐人 - Refers to the person sitting on the north side, traditionally associated with the North Star or the North Pole.
  • 南边坐人 - Refers to the person sitting on the south side, traditionally associated with the South Star or the South Pole.
  • 南斗 - Refers to the Southern Dipper, a constellation in Chinese astronomy.
  • 北斗 - Refers to the Big Dipper, another constellation in Chinese astronomy.
  • 受胎皆从南斗过 - Belief that all souls pass through the Southern Dipper at the time of conception.
  • 见一人生，无量欢喜 - Belief that the Southern Dipper is associated with life and joy.
  • 北斗注杀，见一人死，皆大欢喜 - Belief that the Big Dipper is associated with death and sorrow.
  • 事出《异勿（物）志》 - Reference to the text 'Yi Wu Zhi,' a collection of strange and unusual stories.

Proper Nouns

  • 颜子 - Refers to Yanzi, a historical figure or a person in the story.
  • 《异勿（物）志》 - Refers to the text 'Yi Wu Zhi,' a collection of strange and unusual stories.

[10]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 夜梦见病鬼二虫 - Literal description of a dream where the king sees two ill spirits in the form of insects.
  • 化作二童子 - Describes the transformation of the spirits into two young boys.
  • 针灸所不能及，医药所不能至 - Metaphorical expressions indicating that the illness is in a place where acupuncture and medicine cannot reach.
  • 一如朕梦 - The king's confirmation that the physician's diagnosis matches his dream.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 齐景公 - Refers to Duke Jing of Qi, a ruler of the state of Qi during the Spring and Autumn period.
  • 秦瑗（缓） - Refers to Qin Yuan (or Huan), a renowned physician from the state of Qin, known for his medical skills.
  • 史记 - The *Records of the Grand Historian*, a historical text compiled by Sima Qian, which records the history of China from the legendary Yellow Emperor to the Han Dynasty.
  • 膏肓 - Refers to a part of the human body that is difficult to treat, often associated with incurable diseases.

Proper Nouns

  • 齐景公 - Duke Jing of Qi.
  • 秦瑗（缓） - Qin Yuan (or Huan), the physician.
  • 史记 - The *Records of the Grand Historian*.

[11]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 昔有劉安者，河間人也 - Standard Classical Chinese construction meaning 'Once there was Liu An, a man from Hejian.'
  • 帝命然得歸 - This phrase is somewhat unclear. It could mean 'the emperor allowed him to return home' or 'the emperor ordered him to return home.'
  • 遂能善卜 - Literally 'then became skilled in divination.'
  • 與人占之，上（尚）猶（尤）知未來之事，萬不失一 - Literally 'when he divined for others, he still (or especially) knew future events, never missing once.' The use of '上（尚）' and '猶（尤）' suggests a high level of accuracy and reliability.
  • 合家出后，四合瓦舍忽然大崩落 - Literally 'after the whole family came out, the four-sided tiled house suddenly collapsed.'
  • 公堂舍西头壁下深入三尺，有三个石龙，今日灾祸已过，慎莫发看，发看必令人贫矣 - Describes a hidden stone dragon, and advises against disturbing it.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 劉安 - Liu An was a historical figure known for his involvement in alchemy and the legend of the immortal elixir. He is often associated with the *Huainanzi* (淮南子), a philosophical and scientific text.
  • 河間 - Hejian, an ancient Chinese city, was known for its cultural and political significance.
  • 卜 - Divination, a common practice in ancient China, often involving the interpretation of signs or omens to predict the future.
  • 灾异 - Natural disasters or unusual events that were often interpreted as omens or signs from heaven.
  • 《地理志》 - *The Geography Records*, a historical and geographical text that documented various places and their characteristics.

Proper Nouns

  • 劉安 - Liu An, a historical figure.
  • 河間 - Hejian, an ancient city.
